prompt.instructions:
You are updating a project profile for: {{entity_id}}
Based on the existing profile (if any), new information from trigger files, and recent organizational context from digests, create an updated project profile.
The profile should be written like a project manager's notebook - factual but insightful, tracking the narrative evolution of the project over time.
REQUIRED STRUCTURE:
1. Start with YAML frontmatter in standard format (enclosed in --- delimiters) containing: - name: Full project name - status: active/on-hold/completed/cancelled - timeline: Expected or actual timeline (e.g., "2024-Q4", "Jan-Mar 2024") - key_people: List of person IDs involved (e.g., ["person-sarah-chen", "person-john-doe"]) - teams: List of team IDs involved (e.g., ["team-engineering", "team-sales"]) - dependencies: List of other project IDs this depends on - last_updated: Today's date
Format the frontmatter like this: --- name: Project Name status: active ... ---
2. Then include these sections:
# [Project Name]
## Current Status A concise paragraph describing the current state of the project, including any recent changes or decisions.
## Recent Developments Chronological bullet points of developments actually about THIS project. The <grounded_facts> block (Recent Signals + typed relationships) is the authoritative source. A meeting may touch many projects — do NOT record a development here unless it is tied to this project (a signal/ decision about it, or an explicit statement). Prefer omission over guessing.
## Key People & Roles List people involved and their roles ONLY when grounded in a typed relationship (e.g. works_on, leads) or an explicit statement — not merely because they attended a meeting where this project came up. Example: "- Sarah Chen: Technical lead, driving architecture decisions"
## Teams Involved List the teams and their responsibilities within the project.
## Dependencies & Blockers Any identified dependencies on other projects, systems, or external factors. Current blockers or risks that could impact timeline or success.
## Timeline & Milestones Key dates, phases, and milestones (both planned and completed). Note any timeline changes and their reasons.
## Scope & Objectives What the project aims to achieve and any changes to scope over time.
IMPORTANT GUIDELINES: - CORRECTIONS OVERRIDE EVERYTHING: if the <grounded_facts> block contains a "Corrections (authoritative …)" section, treat each correction as the final truth. It overrides the existing profile, the trigger files, and any derived fact. Never re-introduce a fact a correction contradicts. - ATTRIBUTION IS CRITICAL: this profile is for {{entity_id}} ONLY. Trigger files are shared meetings covering many projects and people. Attribute a development, decision, or person's role to this project ONLY when grounded in <grounded_facts> or an explicit statement — never from mere co-occurrence in the same meeting. Prefer omission over a guess. - Focus on facts and developments, not speculation - Preserve important historical context from the existing profile - Integrate new information naturally without duplication - Use clear, professional language - Reference specific people and teams by their proper IDs in frontmatter - Track how the project narrative evolves over time - Note important decisions and their rationale - Highlight cross-project dependencies and organizational impact
